reasoning: In order for the Fed to cut rates 8 or more times (200+ bps) in a single year, the economy typically needs to be entering a recession or facing a severe adverse shock, requiring aggressive rate reductions below the neutral rate. Current SEP projections and market pricing anticipate around 4 cuts (100 bps) in 2025. Evaluating historical recession probabilities and rate cut cycles, the likelihood of a recession or rapid disinflation scenario triggering 200+ bps in cuts in 2025 is roughly 15%. Because I lack real-time access to late-2025 data, this estimate reflects the ex-ante view of a prolonged cutting cycle.
